essence: David is juggling two clients — a prospective $5,000 engagement he just sent a presentation to, and a 77,000 THB/month client he's behind on deliverables for — while absorbing setup costs (new domain, new AI accounts) and now has one AI agent running recurring research tasks on autopilot.

During the Claude cleanup David flags a fundamental flaw in the agent's approach — running processes without cherry-picking (scanning a 300GB folder), unclear token revocation, and three orphaned shells he had to kill himself — and demands a better recommended path to resolve it quickly.
David directs the agent to find the source of truth itself — re-run the commands (his own output was too large) and check the grep result files that already contain findings.

David asks the agent to update its reports, produce a handover he can carry back to the other system, correct its self-identity (it wrongly believes it is Claude), and explain how to open a new session without going through the item profile.

A persistent usage/limits problem is hitting both of David's paid Claude accounts despite an annual Pro subscription, so he has the brain support document expanded with everything learned — including an instruction to anonymize data — ahead of a support request to Anthropic.

actions.text: Expand brain support document with full diagnostics plus anonymization instruction for the Anthropic support request

essence: David reframes story 5.3 as an upgrade (not a new create-story) informed by the 0.7 'cluster fuck' and Lisa's learnings, requests a comprehensive written handover that can point to files, and refines the system diagram — a Claude Code/Codex/Gemini layer, Paperclip colored differently as an external autonomous system, and a sun-like 'Mythos' badge top-right replacing vague 'wow factor' wording.

actions.text: Produce comprehensive handover message for the story 5.3 upgrade drawing on 0.7 learnings
Revise system diagram: agent-layer ratios, Paperclip as external autonomous system, Mythos badge top-right

essence: David wants one more concept added to the slide deck — a wow-factor tease of his two-week exclusive Anthropic access (without stating it outright) — and is puzzled that the expected access token never appeared, wondering if he must set it up himself.

actions.text: Add one wow-factor slide teasing exclusive Anthropic access, leaving the rest of the deck unchanged

essence: Post-mortem of the Claude Code breakage: write a detailed pain/learnings report into the brains repo, back up ~/.claude instead of wiping it, assess the MacBook Pro via its Ansible config, then reinstall Claude Code linked to the new appydave account (not the old ideasman account) — and weigh adding a 'development review' skill to appydave plugins with Nate querying via Supabase MCP and Lisa/relay integration later.

actions.text: Write detailed pain-and-problems learnings report to the brains folder root
Reinstall Claude Code linked to the appydave account after system assessment via Ansible config
Decide whether to add a development-review skill and update Nate's review responsibilities

essence: David proves the Claude uninstall was incomplete by deliberately launching it — the agent fixated on Homebrew/npm/nvm instead of a legitimate full Claude cleanout — and agrees a fair retest requires a brand-new iTerm session.

Kickoff of the fix: fully uninstall the broken Claude system including hidden app settings and stale session data, verify the Claude session backup David already made, then reinstall connected to the correct account.

actions.text: Verify the existing backup of Claude sessions before uninstall/reinstall

essence: David scraps an over-complex visualization for simple Agentic OS slides — horizontal stack, vertical stack, four development sections (BMAD agents, agent workflow builder, prompt orchestration, AngelEye visualizing + Paperclip autonomous tasks) plus the stack parts not yet integrated for Lars — sets a week-one plan of daily 30-minute integration sessions plus one long BMAD session for Lars's business, and separately requests full Claude removal instructions to reinstall under a new account.

actions.text: Rebuild page one as simple horizontal + vertical Agentic OS slides with the four development sections and Lars integration gaps
Plan week one for Lars: daily 30-minute component sessions plus one long BMAD agent session
Document detailed steps to fully remove Claude/Claude Code and reinstall under a new account

essence: David refuses to close the story: the built list/detail screens are vastly less feature-rich than the reference system's fields and user controls (a gap he's flagged twice before with Lisa the curator), so the original story document must be massively expanded and any botched data schema fixed in-session — plus epic 0.7 wasn't visible in the epics folder because the shell was attached to the old ideasmen account.

actions.text: Relook the story implementation against the original document and expand it to cover all fields and user controls
Fix the data schema and spin up an epic zero to hand data over to the Signal Studio system
Investigate why epic 0.7 was missing from the epics folder (account/usage configuration)

essence: To build the presentation the data must be exported into NotebookLM and a specific prompt crafted to generate the presentation from it — the export path and prompt design are the open questions.

Requirements session for a symmetric Claude skill (built with Anthropic's skill creator, code embedded) that registers SyncThing relay folders — private peer-to-peer Dropbox-style shares — covering the REST API on port 8384 vs plain filesystem, remote access choice (local network / Tailscale / SSH) that needs documenting, handover instructions for machines it can't configure, and staging requirements in a temp folder before building in appydave plugins; relay folders aren't in repos.jsonl yet but may need similar indexing.

actions.text: Capture SyncThing relay-skill requirements in a temp folder, then build the skill with Anthropic's skill creator
Document when to use local network vs Tailscale vs SSH for remote access

essence: Opening questions on SyncThing: how to create a skill or CLI commands to register new synchronization folders, and whether 'private' data belongs as a subfolder of brains or in the new location the agent chose — with the brain updated and ready to push.
